          Join us in making a shared vision of computer science for everyone a powerful reality.

        • Why are students choosing Khoury College? 

          Nearly 100% of new Northeastern graduates are working or in master's programs within nine months. Here are five more reasons Khoury students pick us.

        • Featured

        • Khoury College: Leading in AI innovation and education

          In the age of AI, why pursue a CS degree? From our AI-forward educational ethos to cutting-edge AI research, there actually has never been a better time to join the field. Learn more.

        • Experiential Learning

          It's the Northeastern advantage: Learn about the many experiential learning opportunities that set our students apart.

        • Global Campus Experience

          Study, work, and research at one of our nine network campuses as well as at a wide range of locations on seven continents.

        • Featured

        • The partnership that fuels America

          From our smartphones to the milk we drink, the partnership between government investment, academic research, and industry innovation has driven decades of technological progress and economic growth for the US. Learn how Khoury College is committed to playing a key role.

        • CHI 2026: Khoury researchers set new publishing benchmark

          Khoury researchers are heading to this year's CHI conference in Barcelona with a college-record 41 accepted works, 53 contributors, and five best paper honorable mentions. Learn more from Khoury News.

  • Partners
        • Column one
          • Partnering for Change
          • Industry Partnerships: Engaging Students
          • Industry Partnerships: Collaborating on Research
          • Community Partnerships
        • Featured

        • Hiring a co-op student: What to know

          For more than 500 partners across our global network, co-op students bring fresh perspectives and make an immediate impact. Learn more.

        • Co-op partners see strong demand for recent CS grads

          Companies like Dell, HubSpot, and Notability are leaning into early-career hiring — and seeing real results. With AI making engineers more productive than ever, new hires are contributing immediately, bringing fresh perspectives, and helping companies build better products.

        • Verizon Smart Campus Competition

          Verizon partnered with Northeastern to challenge students to design real-world solutions using 5G and emerging technologies. Explore how the winning teams turned that challenge into feasible, engineering-driven innovations that drew on disciplines from across the campus.

All People

Cheng Tan

(he/him/his)

Assistant Professor

Research interests

  • Computer systems
  • Systems and networking
  • Security
  • Operating systems

Education

  • PhD in Computer Science, New York University
  • MS in Software Engineering, Fudan University — China
  • BE in Software Engineering, Nanjing University — China

Biography

Cheng Tan is an assistant professor in the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University, based in Boston.

Tan's research interests are in systems and security, with a focus on building verifiable outsourced services and certified neural networks for systems. His work has earned him the SOSP Best Paper Award and the Janet Fabri Prize for Outstanding Dissertation.

Before joining Khoury College in 2021, Tan was a research scientist at ByteDance Inc.'s AI Lab.

Labs and groups

  • Systems Research Group

Recent publications

  • Published: October 12, 2025

    TrainVerify: Equivalence-Based Verification for Distributed LLM Training

    Citation: Yunchi Lu, Youshan Miao, Cheng Tan , Peng Huang , Yi Zhu, Xian Zhang , Fan Yang . (2025). TrainVerify: Equivalence-Based Verification for Distributed LLM Training SOSP, 237-253. https://doi.org/10.1145/3731569.3764850
  • Published: March 30, 2025

    PipeLLM: Fast and Confidential Large Language Model Services with Speculative Pipelined Encryption

    Citation: Yifan Tan , Cheng Tan , Zeyu Mi, Haibo Chen . (2025). PipeLLM: Fast and Confidential Large Language Model Services with Speculative Pipelined Encryption ASPLOS (1), 843-857. https://doi.org/10.1145/3669940.3707224
  • Published: December 27, 2024

    Instance-Optimized Mapping with Portfolio Methods

    Citation: Yibo Zhao, Panagiotis Manolios, Cheng Tan . (2024). Instance-Optimized Mapping with Portfolio Methods PACMI@SOSP, 11-16. https://doi.org/10.1145/3704742.3704963
  • Published: September 16, 2024

    Scheduling Splittable Jobs on Configurable Machines

    Citation: Matthew Casey, Rajmohan Rajaraman, David Stalfa, Cheng Tan . (2024). Scheduling Splittable Jobs on Configurable Machines APPROX/RANDOM, 22:1-22:20. https://doi.org/10.4230/LIPIcs.APPROX/RANDOM.2024.22
  • Published: July 10, 2023

    Encrypted Databases Made Secure Yet Maintainable

    Citation: Mingyu Li, Xuyang Zhao, Le Chen, Cheng Tan , Huorong Li, Sheng Wang , Zeyu Mi, Yubin Xia, Feifei Li , Haibo Chen . (2023). Encrypted Databases Made Secure Yet Maintainable OSDI, 117-133. https://www.usenix.org/conference/osdi23/presentation/li-mingyu
  • Published: January 30, 2023

    NNSmith: Generating Diverse and Valid Test Cases for Deep Learning Compilers

    Citation: Jiawei Liu , Jinkun Lin, Fabian Ruffy, Cheng Tan , Jinyang Li , Aurojit Panda, Lingming Zhang . (2023). NNSmith: Generating Diverse and Valid Test Cases for Deep Learning Compilers ASPLOS (2), 530-543. https://doi.org/10.1145/3575693.3575707
  • Published: August 24, 2021

    Building verified neural networks with specifications for systems

    Citation: Cheng Tan, Yibo Zhu, and Chuanxiong Guo. 2021. Building verified neural networks with specifications for systems. Proceedings of the 12th ACM SIGOPS Asia-Pacific Workshop on Systems. Association for Computing Machinery, New York, NY, USA, 42–47. DOI:https://doi.org/10.1145/3476886.3477508
  • Published: July 1, 2021

    Bringing Decentralized Search to Decentralized Services

    Citation: Mingyu Li and Jinhao Zhu and Tianxu Zhang and Cheng Tan and Yubin Xia and Sebastian Angel and Haibo Chen, "Bringing Decentralized Search to Decentralized Services", 15th {USENIX} Symposium on Operating Systems Design and Implementation , 2021, i USENIX Association
  • Published: November 4, 2020

    Cobra: Making transactional key-value stores verifiably serializable

    Citation: Cheng Tan, Changgeng Zhao, Shuai Mu, and Michael Walfish. 2020. COBRA: making transactional key-value stores verifiably serializable. Proceedings of the 14th USENIX Conference on Operating Systems Design and Implementation. Article 4, 63–80.
  • Published: December 19, 2019

    Detecting Incorrect Behavior of Cloud Databases as an Outsider

    Citation: Cheng Tan , Changgeng Zhao, Shuai Mu , Michael Walfish. (2019). Detecting Incorrect Behavior of Cloud Databases as an Outsider CoRR, abs/1912.09018. http://arxiv.org/abs/1912.09018
  • Published: February 26, 2019

    Netbouncer: Active device and link failure localization in data center networks

    Citation: Cheng Tan, Ze Jin, Chuanxiong Guo, Tianrong Zhang, Haitao Wu, Karl Deng, Dongming Bi, and Dong Xiang. 2019. Netbouncer: active device and link failure localization in data center networks. In Proceedings of the 16th USENIX Conference on Networked Systems Design and Implementation (NSDI'19). USENIX Association, USA, 599–613.
  • Published: January 1, 2019

    Taming Distrust in the Decentralized Internet with PIXIU

    Citation: Yubin Xia, Qingyuan Liu, Cheng Tan , Jing Leng, Shangning Xu, Binyu Zang, Haibo Chen . (2019). Taming Distrust in the Decentralized Internet with PIXIU CoRR, abs/1901.06095. http://arxiv.org/abs/1901.06095
  • Published: October 14, 2017

    The efficient server audit problem, deduplicated re-execution, and the web

    Citation: Cheng Tan, Lingfan Yu, Joshua B. Leners, and Michael Walfish. 2017. The Efficient Server Audit Problem, Deduplicated Re-execution, and the Web. In Proceedings of the 26th Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP '17). Association for Computing Machinery, New York, NY, USA, 546–564. DOI:https://doi.org/10.1145/3132747.3132760
